jobs.title: ServiceNow Architect - 277199
jobs.description:
Join a dynamic team driving innovation in service management solutions. We are seeking a ServiceNow Architect to join our client's team on a permanent basis, with a remote working model available across the UK. This role is expected to start in 2026, and is ideal for an experienced Technical Architect looking to make a meaningful impact within a forward-thinking organisation.

**Key Skills Required:**

- In-depth experience with ServiceNow platform architecture and best practices.
- Proven ability to design and implement complex workflows and integrations.
- Strong understanding of IT service management (ITSM) frameworks.
- Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.
- Strong communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with cross-functional teams.

As a part of this team, you will benefit from a comprehensive package that includes a Pension Retirement Savings Plan with contributions, childcare support, medical insurance, and a dental plan.

If you are ready to take the next step in your career and be part of an innovative and inclusive environment, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity.

jobs.title: ServiceNow Implementation Specialist
jobs.description:
UK - ServiceNow Consultants - up to £95,000 + Bonus and Benfits

I'm supporting a leading consultancy that is expanding its ServiceNow capability in the UK. We're seeking ServiceNow Implementation Consultants to join a high-performing and growing practice. HRSD experience is highly advantageous but not essential.

Role Overview

* Contribute to or lead full lifecycle ServiceNow implementations across modules such as ITSM, ITOM, HRSD, SecOps, and CSM
* Work directly with business and technical stakeholders to shape high-quality solutions
* Support the development of best-practice frameworks and platform standards
* Mentor junior colleagues (for senior-level applicants)
* Occasional travel to client sites across the UK, with full flexibility

Requirements

* Hands-on experience with the ServiceNow platform in consulting or delivery roles
* Strong understanding of platform configuration, integrations, and delivery methodologies
* Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
* Relevant ServiceNow certifications (CSA, CIS, CTA, etc.) are a strong plus

What's Offered

* Highly competitive remuneration, dependent on experience and level
* Clear career progression pathways within a growing ServiceNow practice
* Flexible working arrangements
* Opportunity to deliver impactful, enterprise-scale digital transformation projects

If you're exploring your next move within the UK's ServiceNow market, I'd be happy to arrange a confidential discussion.

jobs.title: ServiceNow Architect
jobs.description:
Job Title: ServiceNow Architect
Location: United Kingdom (Hybrid)
Salary: Up to £115,000 + Benefits
Security Clearance: Must be eligible for UK Security Clearance

Overview
A leading UK consultancy is looking for an experienced ServiceNow Architect to join its expanding ServiceNow practice. This role offers the opportunity to lead the architecture and delivery of complex ServiceNow solutions across major digital transformation programmes for both public and private sector clients.

The successful candidate will play a key role in shaping enterprise-scale ServiceNow implementations, with exposure to multiple platform modules including HR Service Delivery (HRSD) and other core ServiceNow capabilities.



Key Responsibilities

*

Define and own the ServiceNow architecture across client engagements.
*

Lead the design and implementation of scalable, secure, and high-performing ServiceNow solutions.
*

Provide architectural oversight across multiple modules, including HR Service Delivery (HRSD) and other platform capabilities.
*

Work closely with client stakeholders, delivery teams, and senior leadership to translate business requirements into technical architecture.
*

Ensure platform design aligns with ServiceNow best practices, governance, and enterprise architecture standards.
*

Provide technical leadership and mentorship to ServiceNow developers and technical teams.
*

Review and approve solution designs, integrations, and custom development.
*

Support pre-sales activities including solution design, estimations, and technical proposals.



Required Skills & Experience

*

Extensive experience working with the ServiceNow platform in a senior technical or architectural role.
*

Strong experience designing solutions across multiple ServiceNow modules (e.g., HRSD, ITSM, CSM, ITOM).
*

Deep understanding of ServiceNow architecture, platform capabilities, and best practices.
*

Experience designing complex integrations using REST/SOAP APIs and third-party systems.
*

Strong stakeholder management and client-facing experience.
*

Experience leading technical teams and guiding delivery on large-scale programmes.
*

ServiceNow certifications such as Certified Technical Architect (CTA), Certified Master Architect (CMA), or equivalent are highly desirable.



What's on Offer

*

Salary up to £115,000 depending on experience
*

Opportunity to lead architecture on major national transformation programmes
*

Access to ServiceNow certifications and advanced training
*

Flexible/hybrid working environment
*

S

jobs.title: ServiceNow HRSD Architect
jobs.description:
Are you a ServiceNow pro who loves shaping great employee experiences. We're looking for someone who can think big picture, dive into the technical detail when needed, and guide both clients and teams toward successful outcomes.

What You'll Be Doing

*

Lead the architecture and design of HRSD solutions across modules like Case & Knowledge Management, Employee Center, Lifecycle Events, and more.
*

Work closely with clients to understand their needs and turn them into scalable, practical ServiceNow solutions.
*

Be the technical go-to person for the team - helping guide consultants and developers during delivery.
*

Stay on top of the latest ServiceNow HRSD updates and features to keep our clients ahead of the curve.
*

Make sure our designs follow best practices and deliver value - not just functionality.

What You Bring

*

Hands-on experience with ServiceNow HRSD - ideally across multiple modules.
*

Solid understanding of ServiceNow architecture, integrations, and platform capabilities.
*

Experience leading end-to-end implementations or solution design for enterprise clients.
*

Great communication skills - you can talk tech with developers and strategy with business leaders.
*

Certifications like CSA, HRSD Implementation Specialist, or CAD are a big plus.
* Eligible for security clearance / ILR

jobs.title: ServiceNow Developer
jobs.description:
Job Title: ServiceNow Developer (HRSD)
Location: United Kingdom (Hybrid)
Salary: Up to £70,000 + Benefits
Security Clearance: Must be eligible for UK Security Clearance

Overview
A leading UK consultancy is seeking a skilled ServiceNow Developer to join its growing ServiceNow practice. This role offers the opportunity to work on large-scale digital transformation programmes for major public and private sector clients. The successful candidate will contribute to the design, development, and implementation of ServiceNow solutions, with a particular focus on HR Service Delivery (HRSD) alongside other platform modules.

Key Responsibilities

*

Design, develop, and implement scalable solutions on the ServiceNow platform.
*

Configure and customise HR Service Delivery (HRSD) and related ServiceNow modules.
*

Build workflows, business rules, integrations, and UI components to meet client requirements.
*

Collaborate with architects, business analysts, and client stakeholders to translate requirements into technical solutions.
*

Develop and maintain integrations with third-party systems using APIs and web services.
*

Support testing, deployment, and ongoing optimisation of ServiceNow solutions.
*

Ensure development aligns with ServiceNow best practices and platform governance.
*

Provide technical guidance and contribute to continuous improvement of the platform capability.

Required Skills & Experience

*

Proven experience developing on the ServiceNow platform.
*

Hands-on experience with HR Service Delivery (HRSD).
*

Experience with additional modules such as ITSM, CSM, or ITOM.
*

Strong scripting capability (JavaScript, Glide API, Flow Designer).
*

Experience with integrations using REST/SOAP APIs.
*

Understanding of ServiceNow data model, workflows, and platform architecture.
*

Experience working within agile delivery environments.



What's on Offer

*

Salary up to £70,000 depending on experience
*

Opportunity to work on major national transformation programmes
*

Access to ServiceNow certifications and training
*

Flexible/hybrid working environment
*

Strong career progression within a growing ServiceNow practice



Please apply to find out more!
